# Implementation Plan: WordPress Social Autopilot

**Branch**: `001-social-autopilot` | **Date**: 2026-06-29 | **Spec**: [spec.md](./spec.md)

**Input**: Feature specification from `specs/001-social-autopilot/spec.md`

**Note**: This plan was generated by `/speckit.plan`. Versions are sourced from
[research.md](./research.md) (Constitution Principle VIII) — never hardcoded.

## Summary

A Next.js (App Router) automation hub that turns one WordPress publish event into
platform-tailored social posts across LinkedIn, Facebook, Instagram, X, TikTok, and YouTube.
A signature-verified webhook ingests the post, Claude Opus 4.8 generates per-platform content
with a backlink, and a durable database-backed queue publishes (or holds for approval) on each
platform with isolated publisher modules, automatic retry, and full audit logging. An
authenticated dashboard provides preview/approve/reject, per-platform auto-publish toggles,
status, manual retry, and OAuth account management.

## Technical Context

**Language/Version**: TypeScript 6.0.3 (strict mode), Node.js 22 LTS runtime

**Primary Dependencies** (confirmed in research.md): Next.js 16.2.9 (App Router) · Prisma 7.8.0

- `@prisma/client` · Zod 4.4.3 · NextAuth.js 4.24.14 (GitHub) · `@anthropic-ai/sdk` 0.107.0
  (model `claude-opus-4-8`) · Tailwind CSS 4.3.2 · Node native `fetch` for social APIs · NovaMira
  MCP for WordPress fallback

**Storage**: PostgreSQL via Prisma (single source of truth — Principle V)

**Testing**: Vitest (unit) + a webhook integration test harness; mocked API responses for
publishers and Claude (Principle VII)

**Target Platform**: Linux server / containerized Node; dashboard served by Next.js

**Project Type**: Web application (Next.js full-stack — App Router routes + server modules +
a queue worker)

**Performance Goals**: webhook ACK < 2s (SC-003); all content work asynchronous

**Constraints**: non-blocking webhook; per-platform isolation; 3× exponential-backoff-with-jitter
retry; encrypted tokens at rest; no secrets in logs

**Scale/Scope**: single WordPress site, single admin, 6 platforms, low-volume (publish-driven)

## Constitution Check

_GATE: must pass before Phase 0 research and re-checked after Phase 1 design._

| Principle                     | How this plan complies                                                                                                                                                                                                                     |
| ----------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ |
| **I. Code Quality**           | TS strict everywhere; no `any` (use `unknown` + Zod); Zod at every boundary (webhook, env, MCP, social API responses, Claude output); small single-purpose modules (one publisher per platform, one service per concern).                  |
| **II. Security**              | HMAC-SHA256 webhook verification with constant-time compare (FR-002); AES-256-GCM token encryption at rest via `lib/crypto.ts` (FR-018); `lib/env.ts` Zod-validates env at startup; no token/secret logging.                               |
| **III. API Integration**      | Isolated `lib/publishers/<platform>.ts` modules behind a shared `Publisher` interface; each handles rate limits, token expiry/refresh, retry; failures logged with context and retried; all independently unit-testable with mocked fetch. |
| **IV. AI Content Generation** | Prompts are version-controlled constants in `lib/ai/prompts/`; every Claude response validated with Zod via `messages.parse()`; per-platform char/tone/format rules enforced; every generated item includes the WordPress backlink.        |
| **V. Database**               | Prisma schema is the single source of truth; every DB op wrapped in error handling; `AuditLog` row per publish attempt/success/failure.                                                                                                    |
| **VI. UX (Dashboard)**        | Real-time per-platform status; preview before publish; per-platform auto-publish toggle.                                                                                                                                                   |
| **VII. Testing**              | Webhook integration tests; Zod schema unit tests; publisher unit tests with mocked APIs; Claude generation tests with mocked responses.                                                                                                    |
| **VIII. Framework Versions**  | All versions confirmed in research.md on 2026-06-29 before coding; pinned in `package.json`.                                                                                                                                               |

**Result**: PASS. No violations requiring Complexity Tracking. One scope-honest deviation
(not a violation): YouTube auto-publish is impossible via API (research.md) and is implemented
as a `MANUAL_REQUIRED` hold, consistent with the spec's YouTube edge case.

### Source Code (repository root) — Section 1: Full App Router folder & file structure

```text
prisma/
├── schema.prisma                      # Section 2: all models (single source of truth)
└── migrations/

src/
├── app/
│   ├── layout.tsx                     # Root layout (Tailwind)
│   ├── page.tsx                       # Redirect → /dashboard (or /signin)
│   ├── globals.css                    # Tailwind entry
│   ├── (auth)/
│   │   └── signin/page.tsx            # NextAuth GitHub sign-in
│   ├── (dashboard)/                   # Auth-guarded group (Section 7)
│   │   ├── layout.tsx                 # Sidebar + auth guard
│   │   ├── dashboard/page.tsx         # Posts list (Server Component)
│   │   ├── posts/[postId]/page.tsx    # Per-post preview/approve/reject
│   │   └── connections/page.tsx       # OAuth connect/disconnect + status
│   └── api/
│       ├── auth/[...nextauth]/route.ts        # NextAuth handler
│       ├── webhooks/wordpress/route.ts        # Section 3: signed webhook receiver
│       ├── oauth/[platform]/start/route.ts    # Section 3/6: begin OAuth
│       ├── oauth/[platform]/callback/route.ts # Section 3/6: OAuth callback
│       ├── connections/route.ts               # GET status; DELETE disconnect
│       ├── posts/route.ts                     # GET posts list (dashboard data)
│       ├── posts/[postId]/route.ts            # GET one post + generated content
│       ├── content/[contentId]/approve/route.ts  # POST approve → enqueue
│       ├── content/[contentId]/reject/route.ts   # POST reject
│       ├── content/[contentId]/retry/route.ts    # POST manual retry
│       └── settings/auto-publish/route.ts     # PATCH per-platform toggle
│
├── lib/
│   ├── env.ts                         # Zod-validated env (Principle II)
│   ├── prisma.ts                      # Prisma client singleton
│   ├── crypto.ts                      # AES-256-GCM encrypt/decrypt tokens
│   ├── auth.ts                        # NextAuth options (GitHub, owner allowlist)
│   ├── webhook/
│   │   └── verify.ts                  # HMAC-SHA256 constant-time verify (FR-002)
│   ├── wordpress/
│   │   ├── schema.ts                  # Zod schema for post payload
│   │   └── novamira.ts                # Section 10: MCP fallback fetch
│   ├── ai/
│   │   ├── client.ts                  # Anthropic client + model constant
│   │   ├── generate.ts                # Section 4: orchestrates per-platform gen
│   │   ├── schemas.ts                 # Zod schemas for Claude output per platform
│   │   └── prompts/                   # Section 4: version-controlled prompt constants
│   │       ├── index.ts
│   │       ├── linkedin.ts   ├── facebook.ts   ├── instagram.ts
│   │       ├── x.ts          ├── tiktok.ts     └── youtube.ts
│   ├── publishers/                    # Section 5: one isolated module per platform
│   │   ├── types.ts                   # Publisher interface + result types
│   │   ├── index.ts                   # registry: platform → Publisher
│   │   ├── linkedin.ts   ├── facebook.ts   ├── instagram.ts
│   │   ├── x.ts          ├── tiktok.ts     └── youtube.ts
│   ├── oauth/                         # Section 6: token management
│   │   ├── config.ts                  # per-platform auth/token URLs + scopes
│   │   ├── tokens.ts                  # get/store/refresh (decrypt→use→re-encrypt)
│   │   └── pkce.ts                    # PKCE helpers (X)
│   ├── queue/                         # Section 8: queue + retry
│   │   ├── enqueue.ts                 # create PublishJob rows
│   │   ├── worker.ts                  # drain loop (separate process)
│   │   ├── process-job.ts             # run one job → publisher → audit
│   │   └── backoff.ts                 # exponential backoff + jitter
│   ├── audit.ts                       # write AuditLog rows
│   └── limits.ts                      # per-platform char limits + truncation (FR-011)
│
├── components/                        # Section 7: dashboard component hierarchy
│   ├── PostList.tsx        ├── PostRow.tsx
│   ├── PlatformPreviewCard.tsx        # preview + approve/reject + status badge
│   ├── StatusBadge.tsx     ├── RetryButton.tsx
│   ├── AutoPublishToggle.tsx
│   ├── ConnectionCard.tsx  ├── ConnectionStatus.tsx
│   └── ui/                            # small Tailwind primitives
│
└── worker/
    └── index.ts                       # entrypoint: starts lib/queue/worker.ts

tests/
├── integration/
│   └── webhook.test.ts                # FR-002/003 receiver tests (Principle VII)
└── unit/
    ├── schemas.test.ts                # every Zod schema (valid + invalid)
    ├── limits.test.ts                 # truncation at word boundary, link preserved
    ├── backoff.test.ts                # delay sequence + jitter bounds
    ├── crypto.test.ts                 # encrypt/decrypt round-trip
    ├── ai/generate.test.ts            # mocked Claude responses
    └── publishers/                    # one test per platform, mocked fetch
        ├── linkedin.test.ts  ├── facebook.test.ts  ├── instagram.test.ts
        ├── x.test.ts         ├── tiktok.test.ts     └── youtube.test.ts
```

**Structure Decision**: Single Next.js App Router project with a co-located standalone queue
**worker** process (`src/worker/index.ts`) sharing `src/lib`. This keeps the webhook non-blocking
(route enqueues; worker publishes) while reusing one codebase and one Prisma client.

## Section 2 — Prisma Models

Full definitions live in [data-model.md](./data-model.md). Summary of the five required models:

- **WordPressPost** — the triggering article (title, content, excerpt, featuredImageUrl, url,
  categories[], tags[], wpPostId unique for dedupe FR-006, sourceComplete, receivedAt).
- **GeneratedContent** — **one row per platform per post** (Clarification): `platform` enum,
  `body`, `hashtags[]`, `link`, `charCount`, `status` enum
  (`PENDING|APPROVED|REJECTED|PUBLISHED|FAILED|MANUAL_REQUIRED`), FK to WordPressPost. Unique
  `(postId, platform)`.
- **PublishJob** — queue row (FK to GeneratedContent, `status`
  `QUEUED|RUNNING|SUCCEEDED|FAILED`, `attempts`, `maxAttempts=3`, `nextRunAt`, `lastError`).
- **PlatformAccount** — **one table, platform-identifier column** (Clarification): `platform`
  unique, `status` enum (`CONNECTED|TOKEN_EXPIRED|DISCONNECTED`), encrypted `accessToken` /
  `refreshToken`, `expiresAt`, `scope`, `autoPublish` boolean, plus platform metadata
  (e.g., `externalAccountId`, `fbPageId`, `igUserId`).
- **AuditLog** — one row per publish attempt/success/failure (FK to GeneratedContent + platform,
  `attempt`, `outcome` enum, `errorContext` JSON sans secrets, `createdAt`).

## Section 3 — API Routes

| Route                              | Method       | Purpose                                                      | Notes                                                                                                    |
| ---------------------------------- | ------------ | ------------------------------------------------------------ | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `/api/webhooks/wordpress`          | POST         | Verify HMAC, dedupe, persist post (only)                     | Returns `202` immediately (FR-003); raw-body read for signature; creates just the `WordPressPost` record |
| `/api/oauth/[platform]/start`      | GET          | Begin OAuth (redirect to provider; PKCE for X)               | `state` CSRF cookie                                                                                      |
| `/api/oauth/[platform]/callback`   | GET          | Exchange code, encrypt+store tokens, set status CONNECTED    |                                                                                                          |
| `/api/connections`                 | GET / DELETE | List connection statuses / disconnect (purge tokens, FR-021) |                                                                                                          |
| `/api/posts`                       | GET          | Dashboard list of triggering posts + per-platform status     | auth-guarded                                                                                             |
| `/api/posts/[postId]`              | GET          | One post + its 6 GeneratedContent previews                   |                                                                                                          |
| `/api/content/[contentId]/approve` | POST         | Approve pending → enqueue PublishJob (FR-015)                |                                                                                                          |
| `/api/content/[contentId]/reject`  | POST         | Mark REJECTED (never publishes)                              |                                                                                                          |
| `/api/content/[contentId]/retry`   | POST         | Manual retry of a failed publish (FR-026)                    | re-enqueues                                                                                              |
| `/api/settings/auto-publish`       | PATCH        | Toggle auto-publish per platform (FR-025)                    |                                                                                                          |
| `/api/auth/[...nextauth]`          | GET/POST     | NextAuth GitHub handler                                      |                                                                                                          |

All non-webhook, non-auth routes require an authenticated owner session. The webhook route is
authenticated by HMAC signature, not session.

**Webhook flow** (kept minimal so the ACK is non-blocking — FR-003): read raw body →
`verify.ts` (reject on mismatch, FR-002) → parse via Zod (`wordpress/schema.ts`) → if
incomplete, `novamira.ts` backfill (FR-005) → upsert `WordPressPost` (dedupe on `wpPostId`,
`generatedAt = null`, FR-006) → respond `202`. **No content rows or jobs are created here.** The
worker's generation pass (Sections 4 & 8) picks up posts where `generatedAt IS NULL`. This avoids
overloading `PublishJob` (which is publish-only) with a generation purpose and keeps the webhook
fast.

## Section 4 — Claude AI Service Module

- `lib/ai/client.ts`: constructs the Anthropic client; exports `MODEL = "claude-opus-4-8"`.
- `lib/ai/prompts/<platform>.ts`: each exports a **version-controlled constant** prompt
  (system + instructions) encoding tone, char limit, hashtag count, hook/CTA, and the mandatory
  backlink rule (Principle IV, FR-010). `index.ts` maps platform → prompt.
- `lib/ai/schemas.ts`: a Zod schema per platform for the model's structured output
  (`{ body: string, hashtags: string[] }`), enforcing array sizes where fixed.
- `lib/ai/generate.ts`: `generateForPost(post)` is invoked by the worker's **generation pass**
  (Section 8) for each `WordPressPost` with `generatedAt IS NULL`. For each platform it builds a
  request via `client.messages.parse({ model: MODEL, output_config: { format: zodOutputFormat(schema) },
thinking: { type: "adaptive" }, ... })`, validates the result, appends the backlink, runs
  `limits.ts` truncation (FR-011), and **creates** the one `GeneratedContent` row for that platform.
  Per-platform failures are isolated (`Promise.allSettled`) so one platform's failure never blocks
  others (FR-016/FR-030). Outcome per platform:
  - usable output, platform supports auto-publish, account `CONNECTED`, auto-publish ON → status
    `APPROVED`, enqueue a `PublishJob`.
  - usable output, manual-approval platform → status `PENDING` (awaits owner approval).
  - **unusable/empty model output** → status `FAILED` with a reason (edge case "generation produces
    unusable output"); no job enqueued.
  - YouTube (no community-post API) → status `MANUAL_REQUIRED`; TikTok without audit / Instagram
    with no featured image → `MANUAL_REQUIRED` per research.md.

  After all six are attempted, set `WordPressPost.generatedAt = now` so the post is not reprocessed.

## Section 5 — Publisher Modules (one isolated module per platform)

`lib/publishers/types.ts` defines:

```text
interface Publisher {
  platform: Platform
  capabilities: { autoPublish: boolean; requiresMedia: boolean }
  publish(content: GeneratedContent, account: PlatformAccount): Promise<PublishResult>
}
type PublishResult =
  | { ok: true; externalId?: string }
  | { ok: false; retryable: boolean; error: string }   // error is secret-free
```

Each `lib/publishers/<platform>.ts` implements `Publisher` using native `fetch`, handling that
platform's auth header, request shape, rate-limit/429 (retryable), token-expiry (trigger refresh
via `oauth/tokens.ts`, then retry once), and mapping responses to `PublishResult`. Per research.md:

- **linkedin**: `POST /rest/posts` with `LinkedIn-Version: 202606`; `commentary` + article.
- **facebook**: `POST /{pageId}/feed` with Page token; `message` + `link`.
- **instagram**: two-step container (`/media` → `/media_publish`); `requiresMedia: true` — if the
  post has no featured image, return a non-retryable skip (surfaced as `MANUAL_REQUIRED`).
- **x**: `POST /2/tweets`; OAuth2 bearer; paid-tier cost noted in docs (~$0.20/link post).
- **tiktok**: `requiresMedia: true`; default inbox/draft flow (`content/init/`) until audited;
  `autoPublish` effectively "queue to drafts".
- **youtube**: `capabilities.autoPublish = false`; `publish()` returns a non-retryable
  `MANUAL_REQUIRED` result (no community-post API).

`lib/publishers/index.ts` is the registry `Record<Platform, Publisher>`.

## Section 6 — OAuth Token Management (storage, encryption, refresh)

- `lib/oauth/config.ts`: per-platform `{ authUrl, tokenUrl, scopes, usesPkce }` (values from
  research.md).
- `start` route: build the provider authorize URL (+ PKCE challenge for X stored in a cookie);
  `callback` route: exchange code → receive tokens → **encrypt** via `crypto.ts` → upsert
  `PlatformAccount` with `status=CONNECTED`, `expiresAt`, `scope`.
- `lib/oauth/tokens.ts`: `getValidAccessToken(platform)` decrypts the stored token; if expired
  or near-expiry, calls the platform refresh endpoint (where supported), **re-encrypts and
  stores the rotated refresh token** (X/TikTok rotate), and returns a usable token. If refresh
  is unavailable/fails (e.g., LinkedIn non-partner), set `status=TOKEN_EXPIRED` and surface a
  reconnect alert (FR-020). Tokens are never logged.

## Section 7 — Dashboard Page Structure & Component Hierarchy

```text
(dashboard)/layout.tsx                 # auth guard (redirect to /signin) + Sidebar
├── dashboard/page.tsx                 # Server Component → fetch posts
│     └── <PostList> → <PostRow>*      # each row: title, time, 6 StatusBadges
├── posts/[postId]/page.tsx            # post detail
│     └── <PlatformPreviewCard>* (×6)  # per platform:
│           ├── <StatusBadge>          # pending/approved/published/failed/manual
│           ├── preview body+hashtags+link
│           ├── Approve / Reject buttons (call content routes)  [if PENDING]
│           └── <RetryButton>          # [if FAILED]
└── connections/page.tsx
      └── <ConnectionCard>* (×6)
            ├── <ConnectionStatus>     # connected / token expired / disconnected
            ├── Connect / Disconnect (OAuth routes)
            └── <AutoPublishToggle>    # PATCH /api/settings/auto-publish
```

Real-time status (FR-024): dashboard pages are Server Components that read current DB state;
client components poll the `/api/posts` endpoints on an interval (or revalidate) so status
transitions (pending→published/failed) appear without a manual refresh.

## Section 8 — Publish Queue & Retry Logic

The worker runs two passes each tick:

- **Generation pass**: selects `WordPressPost` where `generatedAt IS NULL`, calls
  `ai/generate.ts:generateForPost` (Section 4), which creates the six `GeneratedContent` rows,
  enqueues `PublishJob`s for auto-publish + connected platforms, and stamps `generatedAt`.
- **Publish pass**: drains the `PublishJob` queue (below).

- **Enqueue** (`queue/enqueue.ts`): called by the generation pass for auto-publish platforms →
  create `PublishJob (QUEUED)`; manual-approval platforms → job created only on Approve.
- **Worker** (`queue/worker.ts` via `worker/index.ts`): polls `PublishJob` where
  `status=QUEUED AND nextRunAt<=now`, marks `RUNNING`, calls `process-job.ts`.
- **process-job.ts**: resolves the platform `Publisher`; **checks the `PlatformAccount` status —
  if not `CONNECTED`, records a non-retryable "needs reconnect" `AuditLog` + sets content
  `MANUAL_REQUIRED` and continues (edge case "platform not connected at publish time", FR-030)**;
  otherwise fetches a valid token, calls `publish()`, writes an `AuditLog` row (Principle V), and
  on result:
  - success → `GeneratedContent.PUBLISHED`, job `SUCCEEDED`.
  - retryable failure & `attempts < maxAttempts` → increment `attempts`, set
    `nextRunAt = now + backoff(attempts)` (`backoff.ts`: `2^attempt * base` + full jitter),
    job back to `QUEUED`.
  - permanent failure or attempts exhausted → `GeneratedContent.FAILED`, job `FAILED`,
    error context logged (FR-027). One platform's failure never blocks others (FR-030).
- **Manual retry** (`/retry` route) resets `attempts`/`nextRunAt` and re-queues (FR-026).

## Section 9 — Environment Variables (all integrations)

Validated by `lib/env.ts` (Zod) at startup (Principle II). Required:

```text
# Core
DATABASE_URL
NEXTAUTH_URL
NEXTAUTH_SECRET
APP_BASE_URL                      # for OAuth redirect URIs

# Security
WEBHOOK_SECRET                    # HMAC-SHA256 shared secret (FR-002)
TOKEN_ENCRYPTION_KEY              # 32-byte base64 (AES-256-GCM, FR-018)

# Dashboard auth (GitHub)
GITHUB_CLIENT_ID
GITHUB_CLIENT_SECRET
OWNER_GITHUB_LOGIN                # allowlist to the single owner

# Claude
ANTHROPIC_API_KEY

# NovaMira MCP (WordPress fallback)
NOVAMIRA_MCP_URL
NOVAMIRA_MCP_TOKEN
WORDPRESS_SITE_URL

# LinkedIn
LINKEDIN_CLIENT_ID  LINKEDIN_CLIENT_SECRET
# Meta (Facebook + Instagram)
META_APP_ID  META_APP_SECRET
# X / Twitter
X_CLIENT_ID  X_CLIENT_SECRET
# TikTok
TIKTOK_CLIENT_KEY  TIKTOK_CLIENT_SECRET
# YouTube / Google (read/optional; community posting unavailable)
GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID  GOOGLE_CLIENT_SECRET
```

## Section 10 — NovaMira MCP Integration Pattern (WordPress fallback)

- `lib/wordpress/novamira.ts` exposes `fetchFullPost({ wpPostId?, url? })`.
- Pattern: when `wordpress/schema.ts` parsing finds missing required fields (content, excerpt,
  featuredImageUrl, etc.), the webhook handler calls `fetchFullPost(...)`, which invokes the
  **NovaMira MCP** tool (loaded on demand via the MCP tool surface / ToolSearch) to read the
  full post from `WORDPRESS_SITE_URL`. The MCP response is validated with the same Zod schema;
  fields are merged over the partial payload. If the post still can't be retrieved, the run is
  marked failed with a clear reason and nothing is published (spec Edge Case). The MCP
  credentials (`NOVAMIRA_MCP_URL`/`_TOKEN`) come from validated env; responses are typed, never
  trusted raw (Principle I).

## Verification Notes (confirm framework features at implementation)

Versions are pinned in research.md (Principle VIII). These **framework-feature usages** must be
confirmed against the official docs of the pinned version when first used:

- **Tailwind CSS 4** uses a CSS-first config — `@import "tailwindcss"` in `globals.css`, no
  `tailwind.config.js` by default (differs from v3). Confirm the v4 setup for Next.js.
- **Prisma 7** config/CLI conventions (e.g., `prisma.config.ts`, generation output) changed across
  major versions — confirm before scaffolding `schema.prisma`.
- **NextAuth 4** App Router pattern: `app/api/auth/[...nextauth]/route.ts` exporting `{ GET, POST }`
  from `NextAuth(authOptions)`; confirm the v4 route-handler shape and JWT session config.
- **Anthropic SDK 0.107.0**: confirm `client.messages.parse(...)` + `zodOutputFormat` from
  `@anthropic-ai/sdk/helpers/zod`, and `thinking: { type: "adaptive" }` on `claude-opus-4-8`
  (no `budget_tokens`). (Cross-checked against the claude-api skill.)
- **Media hosting**: Instagram and TikTok require the featured image to be a **publicly reachable
  HTTPS URL** (TikTok additionally requires a verified/owned domain) — confirm the WordPress
  `featuredImageUrl` qualifies, else the platform falls to `MANUAL_REQUIRED`.

## Simplification Notes (avoid over-engineering)

- **Queue runner is pluggable.** Keep the DB-backed queue (durability + 3× retry are required),
  but the runner can be **either** a long-lived `worker/index.ts` process **or** a cron-tick route
  (`/api/worker/tick`) that drains due jobs on a schedule. Default to whichever the deployment
  target favors (cron-tick is simpler for serverless). The publisher modules don't change.
- **`PlatformAccount.metadata Json?` is optional.** The known Meta fields use explicit columns
  (`fbPageId`, `igUserId`); `metadata` is for future extension only — omit it if not needed.
- **`components/ui/` primitives are optional.** For a single-admin internal dashboard, inline
  Tailwind utilities are sufficient initially; extract primitives only when reuse appears.

## Testing Coverage (Principle VII — explicit cases)

- **Webhook integration** (`tests/integration/webhook.test.ts`): valid signature, invalid/missing
  signature, tampered body, duplicate `wpPostId`, incomplete payload → NovaMira backfill, <2s ACK.
- **Publisher unit tests** (per platform, mocked `fetch`): MUST cover **success, rate-limit (429),
  token-expiry→refresh→retry, and permanent failure** paths.
- **Claude generation** (`ai/generate.test.ts`, mocked): success, **malformed/unusable response →
  FAILED**, and per-platform isolation (one platform error doesn't block others).
- **Zod schemas** (`schemas.test.ts`): valid + invalid inputs for every schema.
- **Supporting**: `backoff.test.ts` (delay sequence + jitter bounds), `crypto.test.ts` (round-trip),
  `limits.test.ts` (word-boundary truncation, link preserved).

## Database Error-Handling Convention (Principle V)

Every Prisma call goes through a thin wrapper / consistent `try/catch` that logs context
(no secrets) and surfaces a typed error — no unhandled query rejections. Worker passes catch
per-item so one failure never aborts the batch (FR-030).

## Phase 1 — Re-evaluation of Constitution Check

After designing the data model, contracts, and module boundaries: **still PASS.** The design
keeps each principle satisfiable (isolated publishers, Zod at every boundary, audit per attempt,
encrypted tokens, prompts as constants). No new complexity introduced.

## Complexity Tracking

No constitution violations require justification.

| Violation | Why Needed | Simpler Alternative Rejected Because |

## Review & Acceptance Checklist

_Validated during the post-plan audit (2026-06-29). All items confirmed._

### Plan Completeness

- [x] All 10 requested plan sections present (structure, models, routes, AI service, publishers,
      OAuth, dashboard, queue/retry, env vars, NovaMira MCP)
- [x] Technical Context filled with confirmed versions (no NEEDS CLARIFICATION)
- [x] Phase 0 (`research.md`), Phase 1 (`data-model.md`, `contracts/`, `quickstart.md`) generated
- [x] Implementation sequence is coherent: webhook persists post → worker generation pass →
      worker publish pass (no orphaned/ambiguous steps)

### Spec Coverage

- [x] All 30 functional requirements (FR-001..FR-030) map to plan components
- [x] All 8 success criteria (SC-001..SC-008) addressed
- [x] All 9 edge cases handled (incl. not-connected-at-publish, unusable generation output,
      duplicate webhook, signature failure, YouTube no-API)
- [x] All 8 clarifications reflected (truncate, one-row-per-platform, single token table,
      GitHub auth, HMAC, IG Graph API, YouTube hold, backoff+jitter)

### Constitution Compliance (v1.0.0)

- [x] I. Code Quality — strict TS, no `any`, Zod at every boundary, small modules
- [x] II. Security — HMAC constant-time, AES-256-GCM tokens, Zod env at startup, no secret logs
- [x] III. API Integration — isolated per-platform publishers, shared interface, rate-limit/
      expiry/retry, mockable
- [x] IV. AI Content — prompts as constants, Zod-validated output, per-platform rules, backlink
- [x] V. Database — Prisma single source, error-handling convention, audit per attempt/success/
      failure
- [x] VI. Dashboard — real-time (poll) status, preview before publish, per-platform toggle
- [x] VII. Testing — webhook integration; publisher success/rate-limit/expiry/failure; Claude
      malformed-response; Zod valid+invalid (cases named in Testing Coverage)
- [x] VIII. Framework Versions — confirmed in research.md before coding

### Audit Outcomes Applied

- [x] Fixed generation-vs-PublishJob inconsistency (worker generation pass; `generatedAt` marker)
- [x] Added not-connected-account handling at publish time
- [x] Added unusable-generation → FAILED handling
- [x] Added Verification Notes for framework-feature confirmation
- [x] Added Simplification Notes (pluggable runner, optional metadata/ui primitives)
- [x] Named explicit Principle VII test cases
